label: 111_treatment effects_estimators_estimator_inference
description: Researchers use advanced econometric techniques like double/debiased machine learning and flexible difference-in-differences estimators to analyze treatment effects in various contexts, including social experiments. They explore how average treatment effects vary across subgroups.

earliest_articles.title: Clustering, Spatial Correlations and Randomization Inference
earliest_articles.excerpt: Clustering, Spatial Correlations and Randomization Inference It is standard practice in empirical work to allow for clustering in the error covariance matrix if the explanatory variables of interest vary at a more aggregate level than the units of observation. earliest_articles.title: A Score Based Approach to Wild Bootstrap Inference
earliest_articles.excerpt: A Score Based Approach to Wild Bootstrap Inference We propose a generalization of the wild bootstrap of Wu (1986) and Liu (1988) based upon perturbing the scores of M-estimators. This "score bootstrap" procedure avoids recomputing the estimator in each bootstrap iteration, making it substantially less costly to…

earliest_articles.title: Bootstrapping density-weighted average derivatives
earliest_articles.excerpt: Bootstrapping density-weighted average derivatives Employing the \\"small-bandwidth\\" asymptotic framework of Cattaneo, Crump, and Jansson (2009), this paper studies the properties of several bootstrap-based inference procedures associated with a kernel-based estimator of density-weighted average derivatives proposed…

newest_articles.title: The Incredible Flexibility of Moment Matching
newest_articles.excerpt: The Incredible Flexibility of Moment Matching Working Paper 35378 DOI 10.3386/w35378 Issue Date We ask how far the choice of which moments to match can push estimates in misspecified structural models. The answer is: very far. Under regularity conditions, an adversarial researcher informed about the data distribution…

newest_articles.title: Misleading Estimates from Nonlinear Models with a Binary Outcome
newest_articles.excerpt: Misleading Estimates from Nonlinear Models with a Binary Outcome When estimating nonlinear models for binary outcomes, such as probit and logit models, researchers often rely on average partial effects (APEs) to summarize the effect of a regressor. newest_articles.title: With or Without U? Binning Bias and the Causal Effects of Temperature Extremes
newest_articles.excerpt: With or Without U? Binning Bias and the Causal Effects of Temperature Extremes Estimates of climate impacts show that extreme temperatures have large and wide-spread effects. central_articles.excerpt: Double/Debiased Machine Learning for Treatment and Structural Parameters We revisit the classic semiparametric problem of inference on a low dimensional parameter θ_0 in the presence of high-dimensional nuisance parameters η_0.
